name: Sugar Cookies/Basic and Variations
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by Hershey's
Combine the cookie mix, egg, egg yolk, and vanilla in a small bowl. Cover; chill until firm, about 3 hours.
Divide the dough in half. Roll each half to the desired thickness on a lightly floured surface. Cut into desired shapes. Brush with egg white. Place on a greased cookie sheet. Bake at 375°F for 8 to 10 minutes or until lightly browned.
(Cookies spread considerably during baking.) Remove from the cookie sheet and cool on a wire rack. Yield: Approximately 2 dozen cookies.
SURPRISE COOKIES: Makes approximately 1-1/2 dozen cookies. Roll dough on a lightly floured surface to 1/4 inch thick. Cut into 2-1/2 inch circles. Place on a greased cookie sheet. Break a 1/2 pound Hershey's Milk Chocolate Bar into squares. Place a square in the center of each circle and place another circle on top. Seal edges with the tines of a fork. (Reserve remaining chocolate squares for the Pinwheel Cookies, below.) Bake at 375°F for 10 to 12 minutes. Remove from the cookie sheet and cool on a wire rack.
PINWHEELS: Makes approximately 3 dozen cookies. Melt 4 ounces of a Hershey's Milk Chocolate Bar or remaining squares from the Surprise Cookies above; cool. Divide the dough in half. Roll out each half into a rectangle on a lightly floured surface to 1/4 inch thickness. Spread with melted chocolate and roll up dough from the long side. Wrap dough in plastic and refrigerate until well chilled. Cut into 1/4 inch slices. Place on a greased cookie sheet. Bake at 375°F for 8 to 10 minutes. Remove from the cookie sheet and cool on a wire rack.
